description_long: This object consists of two irregular patches of ground, each covered with tufts of dry, yellow-green grass. The larger patch features a dense cluster of grass blades emerging from a grayish, slightly uneven soil base, with some areas more thickly vegetated than others. The smaller patch, set apart from the main one, contains a single, sparser clump of grass on a similarly colored soil base. The grass blades are thin, upright, and vary in height, giving a natural, slightly wild appearance. The edges of both patches are soft and organic, blending gently into the surrounding environment. There is no visible litter or debris, and the surface appears rough and uneven, typical of a natural grass patch in an urban green space.
description: Irregular patches of dry, yellow-green grass growing from grayish soil, with one large and one small clump, both featuring natural, uneven edges.
description_view_0: From this view, the larger grass patch is seen from above with the smaller patch positioned to the left, highlighting the density and spread of the grass tufts.
description_view_1: This angle shows the larger patch's irregular shape and the separation between the two patches, with the smaller patch now behind and to the right.
description_view_2: The view emphasizes the height and density of the grass on the larger patch, with the smaller patch appearing to the right and slightly behind.
description_view_3: Here, the larger patch is seen from a different angle, with the smaller patch in the foreground, showing the sparse grass on the smaller patch and the clustered growth on the larger.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_e: The grass patch transitions gently into the surrounding soil, with no sharp separation, allowing for natural blending with adjacent ground surfaces.
